title: OpenStack Control Plane RabbitMQ Resource Alarm and Connection Churn
dataSources: `index=openstack` `sourcetype="rabbitmq:log"` on controller nodes with file path `/var/log/rabbitmq/rabbit@*.log` or `sourcetype="rabbitmq:metrics"` from management plugin poll
app: Universal Forwarder, RabbitMQ management plugin + optional HEC poller for `/api/nodes`
spl:
index=openstack (sourcetype="rabbitmq:log" OR sourcetype="rabbitmq:metrics") host=*controller* earliest=-1h
| search "file descriptor" OR "memory alarm" OR "disk alarm" OR "low on disk" OR "connection_closed"
| stats count as alarm_events by host
| where alarm_events>=5
| sort -alarm_events
description: OpenStack services depend on RabbitMQ for RPC. File descriptor or memory alarms on the broker throttle API and compute operations cluster-wide. Connection churn logs often precede full broker saturation.
value: Prevents cascading OpenStack outages by treating RabbitMQ alarms as a Sev-1 signal for the control plane.
implementation: Forward RabbitMQ logs only from nodes in the OpenStack control-plane hostgroup (naming convention or CMDB). Tune out known benign restarts. For metrics sourcetype, poll `mem_used`, `fd_used`, `disk_free_limit` and alert on threshold breaches per RabbitMQ clustering guide.
visualization: Single value (nodes in alarm), Timeline (alarm keywords), Table (host, count).
knownFalsePositives: Cluster events during planned hardware refresh, capacity adds, or scheduled maintenance modes.
